documents.name: National ID Card
documents.description: Valid national ID card is sufficient for travel within the EU. No passport required.

documents.name: European Health Insurance Card (EHIC)
documents.description: Provides access to state healthcare in EU/EEA countries. Free to obtain.

notes: No visa required -- freedom of movement within the EU.
No border controls within the Schengen Area.
Your national ID card is sufficient, but carrying a passport is recommended as backup.
EHIC/GHIC covers emergency healthcare, but travel insurance is still recommended.

documents.name: National ID Card or Passport
documents.description: Either is accepted for Schengen area travel.

documents.name: European Health Insurance Card (EHIC)
documents.description: Valid in EEA countries including Switzerland, Norway, Iceland.

notes: No visa required for EU citizens in Schengen-associated countries.
No border controls within the Schengen Area.
Switzerland, Norway, and Iceland are in Schengen but not the EU.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: A passport is required since Brexit. National ID cards are no longer accepted.

documents.name: Travel Insurance
documents.description: EHIC is no longer valid in the UK post-Brexit. Get travel insurance with medical cover.

notes: No visa required for stays up to 6 months (tourism/business).
Passport must be valid for the duration of your stay.
Since Brexit, an ID card alone is NOT sufficient. You MUST have a passport.
EHIC is no longer valid in the UK. Arrange travel insurance with medical coverage.
UK uses pounds sterling (GBP), not euros.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your planned stay.

documents.name: ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization)
documents.description: Required for visa-free entry. Apply at least 72 hours before departure. Costs $21, valid for 2 years.

documents.name: Return/Onward Ticket
documents.description: Proof of onward travel may be requested at immigration.

documents.name: Travel Insurance with US Medical Cover
documents.description: US healthcare is extremely expensive. Ensure your policy covers medical emergencies.

notes: ESTA is required -- apply online at least 72 hours before departure (esta.cbp.dhs.gov).
Passport must be valid for 6 months beyond your stay.
US healthcare costs are astronomical. Travel insurance with medical cover is essential, not optional.
Customs: declare food items, over $10,000 cash, and certain goods on arrival.
TSA PreCheck is not available for foreign visitors. Allow extra time at security.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Must be valid for the duration of your stay.

documents.name: eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization)
documents.description: Required if flying to Canada. Costs CAD $7, valid for 5 years. Apply at canada.ca/eta.

documents.name: Travel Insurance
documents.description: Canadian healthcare is not free for visitors. Get medical travel insurance.

notes: eTA is required when flying to Canada (not needed if arriving by land or sea).
No visa required for EU citizens for stays up to 6 months.
Canadian healthcare is not free for visitors -- travel insurance is essential.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Must be valid for at least 6 months.

documents.name: eVisitor Visa (subclass 651)
documents.description: Free electronic visa for EU citizens. Apply online before departure.

documents.name: Travel Insurance
documents.description: Australia has no reciprocal healthcare with most EU countries. Insurance is essential.

notes: eVisitor visa is free but must be obtained before travel. Allows stays up to 3 months.
Australia has strict biosecurity. Declare ALL food, plant material, and animal products.
No reciprocal healthcare agreement (except Italy, Belgium, and a few others with limited coverage).
Travel insurance with medical evacuation cover is strongly recommended.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Must be valid for the duration of your stay.

documents.name: Return/Onward Ticket
documents.description: Immigration may ask for proof of onward travel.

documents.name: Travel Insurance
documents.description: Japanese healthcare is excellent but expensive for visitors.

notes: No visa required for EU citizens for stays up to 90 days.
You may need to fill out a customs declaration and immigration card on the plane.
Visit Japan Web can be used for pre-entry immigration and customs.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your planned return date.

documents.name: Visa (check requirements)
documents.description: Visa requirements vary by destination. Check your destination country's embassy website.

documents.name: Travel Insurance
documents.description: International travel insurance with medical cover is strongly recommended.

documents.name: Vaccination Certificate
documents.description: Some countries require proof of certain vaccinations (e.g., Yellow Fever).

notes: Check visa requirements for your specific destination well in advance.
Ensure your passport is valid for at least 6 months beyond your return date.
Some countries require proof of vaccination or negative COVID tests.
Register with your embassy if traveling to high-risk areas.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Must be valid for at least 3 months beyond planned departure from the Schengen area.

documents.name: Travel Insurance
documents.description: While not mandatory, travel insurance with medical cover is strongly recommended.

notes: No visa required for US citizens for stays up to 90 days in a 180-day period (Schengen Area).
ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorization System) may be required in the future -- check before booking.
Passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your planned departure from the Schengen area.
There is no Schengen-wide entry stamp requirement. You'll get stamped at first Schengen entry point.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Must be valid for the duration of your stay.

documents.name: Travel Insurance
documents.description: UK NHS does not cover US visitors. Medical insurance is essential.

notes: No visa required for US citizens for stays up to 6 months.
You may need to show proof of return ticket and accommodation at immigration.
UK uses different plug type (Type G) -- bring an adapter.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Post-Brexit, a passport is required. Must be valid for at least 3 months beyond departure date and issued within the last 10 years.

documents.name: Travel Insurance with Medical Cover
documents.description: EHIC is no longer valid. UK GHIC provides limited cover in some EU countries, but travel insurance is essential.

notes: Post-Brexit: UK citizens are limited to 90 days in any 180-day period in the Schengen Area.
Passport must have been issued within the last 10 years AND be valid for at least 3 months beyond your departure date.
UK GHIC provides some healthcare coverage in EU countries, but is not a replacement for travel insurance.
You may be asked to show proof of accommodation, return ticket, and sufficient funds at border control.

documents.name: Valid Passport
documents.description: Ensure at least 6 months validity beyond your return date.

documents.name: Visa (if required)
documents.description: Check visa requirements for your nationality and destination.

documents.name: Travel Insurance
documents.description: Comprehensive travel insurance with medical cover is essential.

documents.name: Vaccination Records
documents.description: Some countries require proof of specific vaccinations.

documents.name: Return/Onward Ticket
documents.description: Many countries require proof of onward travel.

notes: Always check visa requirements for your specific nationality well in advance.
Ensure your passport has at least 6 months validity.
Carry both digital and physical copies of all documents.
